observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has yet to identify the details of the forwarded balance of the Accounts Payable.
Management partially submitted the required documents.
Accounting Office is currently preparing for the lacking documents.
The management posted this position but as to this day still No applicants.
observations.reason: The key positions are still vacant and still handled by personnel in concurrent capacity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


   a. Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile his records of PPE  with
      the physical inventory report, provide depreciation expenses  for  all
      depreciable PPE, and take up all necessary adjustments in  the  books,
      thus, establish the fair balance of the PPE account at any given time.




   b. Require the Supply Officer and Accountant to  maintain  the  Property,
      Plant  and  Equipment  Ledger  Cards  and  record   the   acquisition,
      description, custody, disposal and other information relative  to  the
      assets; and


   c. Require the Supply Officer and Accountant to reconcile  the  inventory
      count report and the accounting records. Investigate and evaluate  the
      differences, adjust the records as appropriate and  submit  a  revised
      RPCPPE based on the reconciliations made.

a. create a committee that will rigorously pursue the collection of prior
      year receivables from  the  identified  accountable  officers/debtors.
      Relative to this, Management shall exhaust  all  possible  efforts  to
      collect the receivables such as, but not limited to instituting  legal
      actions against delinquent borrowers, as applicable;


   b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct  a  thorough  assessment,
      evaluation and investigation of the outstanding  accounts  and  record
      impairment and other adjustments thereof; and


   c.  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  review  the  status  of   the
      receivables and observe the guidelines and procedures on the write-off
      of Dormant Receivable accounts as prescribed in COA Circular  2016-005
      dated December 19, 2016.

We  reiterated  our  recommendation  that  the  inventory  and   disposal
  committees conduct inspection and  appraisal  of  all  the  unserviceable
  properties of the LGU. Thereafter, the Inventory and Inspection Report of
  Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) be prepared as basis  for  the  disposal
  and the dropping of the costs of the disposed properties from  the  books
  of the LGU, thus, present fairly the PPE and Government  Equity  accounts
  in the books.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already adjusted the Outstanding Checks in the books.
Management already posted the Report of Utilization of the BGCM in the LGU Website.
The Municipal Accountant deducted the cash advances from the terminal pay and did not allow any cash advances versus terminal pay.
The Management already submitted the required and necessary supporting documents and justified the observations.
The Management already submitted the lacking documents.
Management already included in 2022 budget for the payments of loan to DA.
The Management already submitted the required supporting documents.
The Post Qualification Reports in 2021 already contain information on the post qualification procedures done and the entities contacted &/or interviewed.
Contractor repaired the physical defects and Management already submitted the necessary documentation to the Office of the Auditor.
Management already submitted all the required lacking documents and justification.
Management already submitted the lacking documents and all the recommendations were already implemented this year.
Management already closed the bank account.
The Post Qualification Reports in 2021 already contain information on the post qualification procedures done and the entities contacted &/or interviewed.
Municipal Accountant submitted the accounts.
Management established a complete record of the LGU’s supplies and PPE’s for this year 2022.
observations.reason: The designated GSO has not yet established a complete record of the LGU’s supplies and PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management enforce collection of the outstanding receivables from the recipients concerned. As regards the receivables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and which collection may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the same following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 in order that the affected accounts shall be presented fairly in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: As of year-end, the account Other Receivables amounting to P262,288.00 remained uncollected. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management identify the creditor LGUs and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Due to LGU amounting to P95,360,739.61 to establish the actual obligation of the LGU and present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Although, the account Due to LGUs as of year-end decreased, still the account was not supported with a subsidiary ledger. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the preparation of plan/budget and reporting of LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Still the format used was not in compliance as to the prescribed form. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that procurements and issuances of gasoline be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized that complete documentation does not only mean the submission of documents but also the completeness of the details thereof.

As to the procurement of gasoline amounting to P76,170.50 from Plus Gold Station, the lacking documents as above listed per COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the corrective and/or explanatory documents for the deficiencies noted in the PR/PO, if any, must be submitted to ascertain the validity and propriety of the disbursement.
observations.reason: Disbursements for gasoline in CY 2019 were still not supported with complete documentation. Hence, this was reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, being the borrower, prepare and maintain records and reports on the receipt and disbursements of the loan received from the Municipal Development Fund Office as required under Article IV of the loan contract for information and audit purposes.
We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of internal control for the procurement, recording and monitoring of Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.reason: Some procurement of supplies and equipment in 2019 were still not supported with complete supporting documents. Also, no report on the inventory of supplies and equipment were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging the cost of gasoline issued to private vehicles to the funds of the Municipality as this is not allowed pursuant to Section 340(e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I. Consider the purchase of motor vehicles to be used for official purposes. With the existence of its own service vehicles, the LGU may minimize or even stop using private vehicles for official travels, thus, ensure or improve efficiency and economy in the use of fuel.
observations.reason: Disbursements for gasoline in CY 2019 were still not supported with complete documentation. Hence, this was reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management  enforce  collection  of
   the outstanding receivables from the recipients concerned. As regards the
   receivables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and  which
   collection may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for
   authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off  the  same  following
   the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under  COA  Circular
   No. 2016-005 in order that  the  affected  accounts  shall  be  presented
   fairly in the books of the Municipality.

   Management Comment:

   Management had already prepared a request for  write-off  of  receivables
   which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and will be  submitted
   to the Office of the Auditor.

   Auditor’s Rejoinder:

   Once the request will be submitted to the  Office  of  the  Auditor,  the
   necessary audit procedures will be undertaken.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management  identify  the  creditor
  LGUs and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Due to LGU  amounting
  to P76,547,137.85 to establish the  actual  obligation  of  the  LGU  and
  present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.

  Management Comment:

  Management thru the  Municipal  Accountant  explained  that  the  account
  pertains to tobacco fund which are for distribution to various  barangays
  and/or various tobacco farmers based on the list provided by the National
  Tobacco Administration (NTA).

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  recognize  payments  of
   mobilization fees to contractors as “Advances to Contractors” instead  of
   as  Other  Payables.  Accordingly,  she  should  take  up  the  necessary
   adjustments for the payments amounting to P1,601,799.48 to present fairly
   the affected accounts in the books.

   Management Comment:

   Management thru the Municipal Accountant accepted the  audit  observation
   and committed to implement the audit recommendations. As of this writing,
   the Municipal Accountant had  already  made  the  necessary  adjustments.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU be
   supported with complete documentation as  prescribed.  It  is  emphasized
   that completeness of documentation means not only the submission  of  the
   documents but also  the  completeness  and  consistency  of  the  details
   thereof.

   In this regard, the lacking and necessary explanatory documents  for  the
   procurement  of  goods  amounting  to  P2,637,828.91  be  submitted   for
   verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursement may be disallowed in audit.




   Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations. The Accounting Office is currently  preparing  the
   lacking and necessary explanatory documents for submission to the  Office
   of the Auditor.


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


   Upon verification, once the lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents
   are still incomplete, the necessary audit action/s will be undertaken  by
   the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management stop the issuance of gasoline to privately
   owned motor vehicles.  In  case  official  vehicles  are  not  used,  the
   officials and employees concerned may reimburse  the  customary  mode  of
   transportation for their official travels instead.

   As regards the procured gasoline amounting to P P1,034,390.08, Management
   submit the lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents/information  for
   verification. Should the required documents/information be not  submitted
   or found not in order, the disbursements may be disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   explanatory  documents  for  the  procurement  of  goods   amounting   to
   P656,051.92 for verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or
   found not in order, the disbursement may be disallowed in audit.




Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations. The Accounting Office is currently  preparing  the
   lacking and necessary explanatory documents for submission to the  Office
   of the Auditor.


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


   Upon verification, once the lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents
   are still incomplete, the necessary audit action/s will be undertaken  by
   the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Technical Working Group  (TWG)  of  LGU  Cabagan,
   Isabela  conduct  the   post-qualification   following   the   prescribed
   procedures to serve its purpose,  thus,  ensure  that  all  projects  are
   awarded to and undertaken by bidders/contractors which do not only  offer
   the lowest bid but which are also found complying and responsive  to  all
   the pertinent requirements and conditions.

   Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations. As of  this  writing,  the  TWG  of  LGU  Cabagan,
   Isabela had been undertaking the proper conduct of the post-qualification
   process.

We recommended that Management ensure that payments of cash assistance be
   made on time to serve the intended purposes thereof.

   Also, the Municipal Accountant ensure that the obligations  and  payments
   of cash assistance as well as other transactions be taken up in the books
   correctly.   In   this   regard,   she   should   make   the    necessary
   adjusting/correcting  entries  for  the  cash  assistance  amounting   to
   P60,000.00 to present fairly the affected accounts in the books.

   Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations. As of this writing, the Municipal  Accountant  had
   already made the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management prepare the Local Disaster Risk  Reduction
   and Management Plan (LDRRMP) in accordance with the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Accordingly, the Quick Response Fund  (QRF)
   should  be  30%  of  the  total  budget,  and  the  plan  should  include
   information on the  list  of  projects  and  activities  charged  to  the
   unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. This is to ensure that  the  purpose
   of the fund may be maximized and the audit of the utilization thereof may
   be effective.

   Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations. For the  year  2020,  they  already  prepared  the
   required format of their LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up funds granted to the
   Department  of  Education-Cabagan  District  Office  or  its   authorized
   representatives for specified purposes as “Due from NGAs” instead  of  as
   direct expenses to present fairly the accounts in the books as well as to
   establish  accountability  and  demand  for  liquidation.  Moreover,   we
   recommend that Management ensure that the fund transfers be made on  time
   to serve the intended purposes thereof.

   Management Comment:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management submit the required lacking documents  for
   the procurement of medical and laboratory equipment costing P1,800,000.00
   to prove the validity, legality and propriety of the transaction.  Should
   the required documents be not  submitted  or  found  not  in  order,  the
   disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

    Management Comment/s:

   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit  recommendations.  As  of  this  writing,  Management  had  already
   submitted the required lacking documents.


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


   Upon verification, once the lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents
   are still incomplete, the necessary audit action/s will be undertaken  by
   the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management heed  to  the  advice  of  the  Provincial
   Budget Officer to create the position of Municipal  Government  Assistant
   Department Head-SG 22 under the Office of the  Municipal  Engineer.  Once
   the position is created and  approved  by  the  local  sanggunian,  Engr.
   Cabauatan be appointed to the new position and the position  Engineer  IV
   be dissolved.

   In this regard, not until the new position is created and not  until  Mr.
   Cabauatan is appointed to said position, the appropriation for  the  RATA
   of  Engr.  Cabauatan  as  Engineer  IV  is  not  valid  and,  thus,   the
   corresponding disbursements made may be disallowed in audit.

   Management Comment:

   Management had already stopped the payment of RATA to the said official.

We recommended that Management submit justification on  the  transactions
   made with the suppliers which proprietors are related by consanguinity or
   affinity with the LGU being the procuring entity.

   Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant disclose information  on
   related parties and related party transactions in the Notes to  Financial
   Statements to strengthen accountability and to  facilitate  understanding
   of the financial statements of  the  Municipality  by  all  readers/users
   thereof.

   Management Comment:

   Management had already disclosed its related party transactions and  were
   presented in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2019.

We recommended that Management prioritize the filing-up of the  positions
   of Municipal Assessor, Municipal Budget Officer,  and  Municipal  General
   Services  Officer  to  have  personnel  to   perform   the   duties   and
   responsibilities on a full-time capacity, thus, may facilitate  the  flow
   of operations and strengthen the internal control system of the LGU.

   Management Comment:

   Management had already submitted the justification/s  pertaining  to  the
   audit observation and recommendations.

We recommended that Management submit justification on  the  transactions
   made with the suppliers which proprietors are related by consanguinity or
   affinity with the LGU being the procuring entity.

   Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant  disclose  information
   on related parties  and  related  party  transactions  in  the  Notes  to
   Financial Statements  to  strengthen  accountability  and  to  facilitate
   understanding of the financial statements  of  the  Municipality  by  all
   readers/users thereof.

   Management Comment:

   Management had already disclosed its related party transactions and  were
   presented in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2019.

We recommended that Management submit the following for verification:

    • Copy of the office orders creating the Inspection  Committee  of  LGU
      Cabagan, Isabela in 2018 and 2019, if any; and
    • A justification why Mr. Guingab was designated as BAC Chairman,  Head
      of the Inspection Committee, and General  Services  Officer/Accepting
      Officer at the same time until August 2018.

   Also, we recommended that, in the creation of office  orders,  Management
   may  consider  proper  delineation  of  functions  and   avoid   multiple
   functions. This is to ensure check and balance and proper accountability,
   thus, stronger internal control.

   Management Comment:


   Management had already submitted copy of the office orders  creating  the
   Inspection Committee of the LGU and the justification why Mr. Guingab was
   designated as BAC Chairman, Head of the Inspection Committee and  GSO  at
   the same time.

We reiterated our recommendation that  the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
   timely  submission  of  accounts.  Accordingly,  she  should  cause   the
   immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of  the  LGU  for  2019,
   otherwise, the corresponding disbursements may be  disallowed  in  audit.
   Also, the salaries of the officials concerned maybe withheld.




   Management Comment:


   Management  accepted  the  audit  observation  and  committed  to  submit
   regularly the monthly financial reports of the Municipality. As  of  this
   writing, accounts in 2019 and January 2020 were already submitted to  the
   Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurement activities  of
   the Municipality be supported with complete documentation as  prescribed.
   It is emphasized that complete  documentation  does  not  only  mean  the
   submission of documents but also the completeness and consistency of  the
   details thereof.

   In this regard, the lacking documents and the necessary corrective and/or
   explanatory documents  for  the  deficiencies/inconsistencies  noted  re:
   procurement of various equipment for ecological  solid  waste  management
   costing P1,037,000.00  be  submitted  for  verification,  otherwise,  the
   disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

   Management Comment:


   Management accepted the audit observation and committed to implement  the
   audit  recommendations.  As  of  this  writing,  Management  had  already
   submitted the required lacking documents.


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


   Upon verification, once the lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents
   are still incomplete, the necessary audit action/s will be undertaken  by
   the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer: Record correctly his collections and deposits in the cashbook to reflect the correct balances of his accountability at any given time.; Reconcile the cashbook balances for collections with the cash on hand.; and Reconcile the cashbook balances for collections with the subsidiary ledger maintained at the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to ensure the validity and propriety thereof at any given time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the component transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Other MOOE amounting to P17,719,276.25 to establish the reliability thereof and present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: A listing of the component transactions were disclosed in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit its program on the grant of livelihood assistance to individuals. As regards the payment of FA amounting to P60,000.00 in 2018, Management submit the lacking documents. Should the required documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Some disbursements for financial assistance for livelihood projects in 2019 were still not supported with complete supporting documents i.e. letter-request from the recipients and a copy of the duly approved livelihood program of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the LGU be allotted and be used for priority development programs, projects and activities as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to fully realize its intended purpose.

We recommended that Management: Prepare the LGU’s Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report using the prescribed format under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01. The gender issues to be addressed by the programs, projects and activities must be based on the LGU’s sex disaggregated data (SDD); Ensure that the GAD PPAs are implemented according to plan/budget and that any changes in the budget must be duly approved and documented.; and As regards the excess disbursements of P176,978.83, Management submit an explanation or justification to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance thereof.

We recommended that Management stop the grant of RATA to the Chief PNP, Cabagan Station. As to the RATA granted in 2018, Management should submit a written justification and/or legal basis on the allocation and payment thereof to avoid any possible suspension or disallowance of the corresponding disbursements.

We recommended that Management stop the grant of clothing allowance to workers whose services are engaged without employer-employee relationships. As to the clothing allowance granted to job orders in 2018 amounting to P2,500.00 each or a total of P200,000.00, Management should submit a written justification and/or legal basis to avoid any possible suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that Management ensure that payments of wages to contractual/casual workers are based on actual and complete services rendered and are supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity and propriety thereof.

In this regard, the required lacking documents for the payments of wages amounting to P3,218,784.59 be submitted for verification. Should the required documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the rates of travel expenses as prescribed under EO No. 298 and the required documentary requirements as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to prove the validity and accuracy of the disbursements.
observations.reason: Some payments for travelling expenses of various officers and employees in 2019 were still not supported with complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, she should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.reason: Accounts in 2019 were still not submitted within the prescribed period. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

observations.reason: The balance of outstanding Other Receivables as at December 31, 2018 even increased. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officer:

      • Record correctly his collections and deposits in  the  cashbook  to
        reflect the correct balances of his  accountability  at  any  given
        time.;


      • Reconcile the cashbook balances for collections with  the  cash  on
        hand.; and


      • Reconcile the cashbook balances for collections with the subsidiary
        ledger maintained at the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular No. 97-002  on  the  granting  and  liquidation  of  cash
   advances to ensure the validity and propriety thereof at any given  time,
   as follows:

      • A cash advance should  be  granted  only  for  a  legally  specific
        purpose/s.;


      • No additional cash advance should be granted  unless  the  previous
        cash advance has already been liquidated or accounted for.; and


      • A cash advance must be liquidated immediately after  the  purpose/s
        for which it was granted has been served or completed.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management  enforce  collection  of
   the outstanding receivables from the recipients concerned. As regards the
   receivables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and  which
   collection may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for
   authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off  the  same  following
   the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under  COA  Circular
   No. 2016-005 in order that  the  affected  accounts  shall  be  presented
   fairly in the books of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management  identify  the  creditor
   LGUs and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Due to LGU  amounting
   to P95,360,739.61 to establish the  actual  obligation  of  the  LGU  and
   present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  identify  the  component
   transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger for the  account  Other  MOOE
   amounting to P17,719,276.25 to  establish  the  reliability  thereof  and
   present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management  revisit  its  program  on  the  grant  of
   livelihood assistance to  individuals.  As  regards  the  payment  of  FA
   amounting to P60,000.00 in 2018, Management submit the following:

    • Duly signed and notarized Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);


    •  Duly  approved  Livelihood  Program  prescribing  the  coverage,  the
      intended beneficiaries, the source of funding, the qualifications, the
      type of livelihood projects,  the  corresponding  amount  of  FA,  the
      responsibility/accountability of the recipients, and the monitoring of
      the program results;


    • Letter-requests of the recipients; and


    • Explanation why the FA were charged against the 20%  Development  Fund
      in spite of the absence of an allocation for the purpose in  the  2018
      budget for 20% Development Fund.

   Should the required documents be not submitted or found not in order, the
   disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20%  Development  Fund  of
   the LGU be allotted  and  be  used  for  priority  development  programs,
   projects and activities as prescribed  under  DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum
   Circular No. 2017-1 to fully realize its intended purpose.

   As regards  the  project-Painting  of  Tricycles  costing  P2,350,000.00,
   Management submit a justification for its inclusion in the PPAs under the
   20% Development Fund and information on the  status  of  the  project  to
   avoid the  possible  suspension  or  disallowance  of  the  corresponding
   disbursements.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular No.  2012-002  on  the  preparation  of  plan/budget  and
   reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly, to ensure effective and timely audit of
   the fund, the LDRRMO:

    • prepare the LDRRMP/LDRMFIP following the prescribed format;
    • prepare the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of  DRRMF
      using the  prescribed  format  (certified  correct  by  the  Municipal
      Accountant); and
    • submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of
      DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:

    • Prepare the LGU’s Annual GAD Plan and Budget  and  GAD  Accomplishment
      Report using  the  prescribed  format  under  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint
      Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01. The gender issues to be addressed  by
      the programs, projects and activities must be based on the  LGU’s  sex
      disaggregated data (SDD);


    • Ensure that the GAD PPAs are implemented according to plan/budget  and
      that any changes in the budget must be duly approved and  documented.;
      and


    • As regards the excess disbursements of P176,978.83, Management  submit
      an explanation or justification to avoid the  possible  suspension  or
      disallowance thereof.

We recommended that Management stop the grant of RATA to the  Chief  PNP,
  Cabagan Station. As to the RATA granted in 2018, Management should submit
  a written justification and/or legal basis on the allocation and  payment
  thereof  to  avoid  any  possible  suspension  or  disallowance  of   the
  corresponding disbursements.

We recommended that Management stop the grant of  clothing  allowance  to
  workers   whose   services   are   engaged   without    employer-employee
  relationships. As to the clothing allowance granted to job orders in 2018
  amounting to P2,500.00 each or a total of P200,000.00, Management  should
  submit a written justification and/or legal basis to avoid  any  possible
  suspension or disallowance.

We  recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  payments  of  wages   to
   contractual/casual workers are based  on  actual  and  complete  services
   rendered and are supported with complete documentation as  prescribed  to
   prove the validity and propriety thereof.

   In this regard, the required lacking documents for the payments of  wages
   amounting to P3,218,784.59 be  submitted  for  verification.  Should  the
   required  documents  be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in  order,   the
   disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management  strictly  observe  the  rates  of  travel
   expenses as prescribed under EO No.  298  and  the  required  documentary
   requirements as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to  prove  the
   validity and accuracy of the disbursements.

   In this regard, the LGU officials  and  employees  concerned  submit  the
   lacking and necessary corrective/explanatory  documents  to  support  the
   travel expenses amounting to P113,790.20 for verification, otherwise, the
   corresponding amounts be refunded.

We recommended that Management ensure that procurements and issuances  of
   gasoline be supported with complete documentation as  prescribed.  It  is
   emphasized that complete documentation does not only mean the  submission
   of documents but also the completeness of the details thereof.

   As to the procurement of gasoline amounting to P76,170.50 from Plus  Gold
   Station, the lacking documents as above listed per COA Circular No. 2012-
   001 and the corrective and/or explanatory documents for the  deficiencies
   noted in the PR/PO, if any, must be submitted to ascertain  the  validity
   and propriety of the disbursement.

   On the issuance  of  the  gasoline  to  the  SB  members,  the  following
   documents should be submitted for verification:

    • SB Resolution authorizing the augmentation of travel allowance in  the
      form of gasoline;
    • Itinerary of Travel or List of Activities  during  the  month  of  the
      claimants, duly approved;
    • Gasoline Issue Slips showing the plate  number  and  the  quantity  of
      gasoline to be issued; and
    • Summary of Gas Issed, duly signed/approved, showing the plate numbers
      and the purposes of travel.

We recommended that Management, being the borrower, prepare and  maintain
   records and reports on the receipt and disbursements of the loan received
   from the Municipal Development Fund Office as required under  Article  IV
   of the loan contract for information and audit purposes.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of  internal
   control for the procurement, recording and  monitoring  of  Supplies  and
   Property, Plant and Equipment, as follows:

      • Replacement of damaged parts of vehicles/equipment be supported with
        Waste Material Reports (WMR).;


      • The procured items be received, accepted and recorded in  the  Stock
        or Property Cards by the Supplies/Property Officer. Stock Cards  and
        Property Cards be maintained for  each  type  of  Supplies  or  PPE,
        respectively.;


      • The Supply/Property Officer issues supplies/PPE  to  the  requesting
        office  or  end-users.  The  issuances  should  be  supported   with
        Requisition and Issue Slips. The issuance of small items not  to  be
        considered as PPE be covered with Inventory  Custodian  Slips  (ICS)
        and  issued  PPE  be  supported  with  Acknowledgment  Receipt   for
        Equipment (ARE).;


      • The Accounting Office should also maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and
        PPE Ledger Cards for supplies and PPE, respectively.; and


      • The Supply/Property Office and  Accounting  Office  reconcile  their
        respective records regularly.

We reiterated our recommendation that  the  Municipal  Accountant  ensure
   timely  submission  of  accounts.  Accordingly,  she  should  cause   the
   immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for  2018  to
   facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible
   suspension  or  disallowance  of  the  same   and   the   imposition   of
   administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant take
up funds granted to other
Municipalities for
specified purpose as “Due
from LGU’s” for fair
presentation of accounts in
the books as well as to
establish accountability and
demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant
reconcile the reciprocal
accounts Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other
Funds, thus, fairly present
the accounts in the
financial statements.

We reiterated the
recommendation that
Management identify the
creditor LGUs and set up a
subsidiary ledger for the
account Due to LGU
amounting to P49,357,213.00
to establish the
reliability thereof and the
actual obligation of the
LGU.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant has
not yet established a
subsidiary ledger or a
listing of the LGU creditors.
Hence, reiterated in
Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant take
up the necessary
adjustments in the books of
the LGU to fairly present
the balances of the
affected accounts.

We recommended that
Management ensure that all
fund transfers to NGOs/POs
be supported with complete
documentation as
prescribed.
In this regard, the lacking
and necessary corrective
and/or explanatory
documents for the
deficiencies/inconsistencie
s noted on the fund
transfer to NGO-Ipa Kalan
Producers Association
amounting to P509,550.00 be
submitted for verification.
Should the documents be not
submitted or found not in
order, the disbursement may
be suspended or disallowed
in audit.

We recommended that
Management ensure that all
procurement of
infrastructure projects of
the LGU be supported with
complete documentation as
required under COA Circular
No. 2012-001. In this
regard, the lacking
documents for the
procurement of
infrastructure
project-Improvement of
Aggabao Hall amounting to
P4,606,934.52 be submitted
for verification.

We recommended that the
Municipal Engineer’s Office
post or display a tarpaulin
signboard for its
infrastructure projects at
the project locations
showing all the essential
features/elements of the
projects as required under
COA Circular No. 2013-004,
thus, promote proper
information and
transparency.

We recommended that
Management ensure that the
20% Development Fund of the
LGU be allotted and be used
for priority development
programs, projects and
activities as prescribed of
under DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1 to fully realize its
intended purpose.
As regards the
appropriations for 2017 in
the amount of
P3,900,000.00, Management
submit an explanation
and/or  justification for
the inclusion of the
identified PPAs under the
20% Development Fund to
avoid the possible
suspension or disallowance
of the corresponding
disbursements.
observations.reason:
One project for 20%
Development Fund in 2018
was not a capital
expenditure. Hence,
reiterated in Part II of
this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management remit
contributions, premiums and
loan amortizations which
are deducted from the
salaries and wages of
officials and employees
within the prescribed
period to avoid the
incurrence of penalty
charges.

We recommended that
Management ensure that all
procurements chargeable
against the Special
Education Fund of the LGU
be supported with complete
documentation as
prescribed.
As to the disbursement
amounting to P44,490.00,
the lacking and the
necessary corrective/
explanatory documents, if
any, be submitted to
ascertain the validity and
propriety thereof.
Otherwise, the transaction
may be suspended or
disallowed in audit.

We recommended that
Management appropriate 1%
or even more than 1%, if
necessary, of the LGU’s
Internal Revenue Allotment
for the strengthening and
implementation of the
programs, projects and
activities of the Local
Council for the Protection
of Children.

We recommended that
Management ensure that
payments of terminal leave
benefits (TLB) be supported
with complete documentation
as prescribed to prove the

the disbursements.
Correspondingly, the
lacking documents and the
necessary corrective
documents for the
deficiencies noted for the
payment of the TLB of Ms.
Manuel be submitted to
avoid the possible
suspension or disallowance
thereof.

We recommended that
Management ensure that all
procurement activities of
the Municipality be
supported with complete
documentation as
prescribed.
In this regard, the lacking
and necessary explanatory
documents for the
disbursements amounting to
P1,574,347.69 be submitted
for verification. Should the
documents be not
submitted or found not in
order, the disbursements
may be suspended or
disallowed in audit.

Some procurements in 2018
were still not supported
with complete
documentation.

Management ensure that all
procurements of the LGU are
supported with complete

In this regard, the lacking

with complete
documentation.

We recommended that
Management submit the
purchase orders, government
contracts and their

and effective review

and government contracts for
most procurement activities

submitted to the Office of

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management require the
contractor to re-install the
pump to a place where
there is available water to
make it fully operational
and serve its intended
purpose for the benefit of
the residents of the
Barangay.

We recommended that
Management ensure that the
ESWM plan and budget be
implemented/used to fully
realize the intended
purpose/s of the identified
programs, projects and
activities.
observations.reason:
Some PPAs for Solid Waste
Management were still not
implemented in 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the
recommendation that
Management formulate a
policy implementing
guideline on the grant of
financial assistance for
livelihood projects to
private individuals. The
guideline includes the
requirements of
letter-request, barangay
certification, social case
study report, project
proposal and Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) clearly
stating the
responsibilities/obligation
s of the recipients to
ensure that the financial
assistance is granted and
used only for the intended
purpose and duly accounted
for.
observations.reason:
Some financial assistance
for livelihood projects were
still given to private
individuals in 2018 without
clear provisions and
with incomplete
documentation.
Hence, reiterated in Part II
of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management require all
persons responsible to
settle the suspensions
amounting to P1,919,005.07
to clear them from their
accountabilities and, thus,
avoid the issuance of audit
disallowances. Accordingly,
the accountable officials
submit the requirements
indicated in the Notices of
Suspension (NS).
observations.reason:
Two audit suspensions
unsettled as of December
31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
management maximize the use
of burley and tobacco
excise tax so that the
tobacco farmers would be
fully benefitted from said
fund.
observations.reason:
The LGU has utilized only a

collection in 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management formulate a policy implementing guidelines for the granting of financial assistance and the basic requirements like letter request, barangay certification, and social case study be attached to the disbursement vouchers.
We also recommended that the following information be included in the Memorandum of Agreement: Complete postal address of the grantee, provision as to collectability/liquidation of grant, and monitoring of the success of the program.
observations.reason: Financial assistance for livelihood projects were still granted to private individuals in 2017 without clear provisions and guidelines. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management  enforce  collection  of  the  outstanding
receivables from the recipients concerned.  As  regards  the  receivables
which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and  which  collection
may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for  authority
to write-off the same from the Commission on Audit following the specific
guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No.  2016-005,
thus, fairly present the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take  up  funds  granted  to
other Municipalities for specified purpose as “Due from LGU’s”  for  fair
presentation  of  accounts  in  the  books  as  well  as   to   establish
accountability and demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  the  reciprocal
accounts Due from Other Funds  and  Due  to  Other  Funds,  thus,  fairly
present the accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated the recommendation that Management  identify  the  creditor
LGUs and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Due to LGU  amounting
to P49,357,213.00 to establish the reliability  thereof  and  the  actual
obligation of the LGU.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  take  up  the  necessary
adjustments in the books of the LGU to fairly present the balances of the
affected accounts.

We recommended the following:
That the Municipal Accounting Office should exert effort to locate the records to identify the creditor LGUs.

The submission of the Utilization Plan of the unutilized balance of the fund.

Make representation with DBM Regional Office to furnish the LGU copy of DA for the release of the excise tax in the prior years.
observations.reason: The LGU already has a utilization plan for the fund. However, still the required subsidiary ledger or listing of the creditor LGUs was not yet established by the Municipal Accountant. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer require the submission of duly accomplished daily trip tickets before the use of government vehicles. Check disbursements for repair and maintenance of LGU vehicles in the total amount of P835,598.69 were not supported with complete documentation that would prove the regularity and legality of expenditures which is not in accord with existing rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement and that for future similar transaction, the required supporting documents should accompany the Disbursement Voucher before payment to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office conduct a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.
observations.reason: Issued Notice of Suspension

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement and that for future similar transaction, the required supporting documents should accompany the Disbursement Voucher before payment to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office conduct a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.
observations.reason: Repairs of LGU vehicles costing P128,050.00 in 2017 were still not supported with complete documentation. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned office submit the required documents as mandated by COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Copies of Purchase Order and Government Contracts of some projects were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor for review. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts for post-audit.
observations.reason: DVs and supporting documents for 2017 were still not submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management expedite the implementation of the project so that beneficiaries would obtain maximum benefits from the government funds and to cope up with the timetable set by the donor agency.
We recommended that the management maximize the use of burley and tobacco excise tax so that the tobacco farmers would be fully benefitted from said fund.
observations.reason: The LGU has utilized only a portion of the excise tax collection in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the persons liable to immediately settle the remaining suspensions and disallowances of P145,560.00. Settlement of the audit suspensions is through compliance with the requirements indicated for our evaluation. Items suspended in audit which are not settled within ninety (90) days from receipt shall become a disallowance pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts prescribed for use under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Audit Disallowances not appealed within 6 months becomes final and executory.
observations.reason: Refunded per OR No. 9302052 dated January 20, 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Service Officer requires the submission of duly accomplished daily trip tickets before the use of government vehicles
observations.reason: reiterated as Finding No. 2 of this report

observations.recommendation:
Since management asserted that the above amount pertains to congressional excise taxes of prior years that has been recorded under the Due to LGUs Account, we recommend the following:
That the Municipal Accounting Office should exert effort to locate the records to identify the Creditor LGUs.
The submission of the Utilization Plan of the unutilized balance of the fund.
Make representation with DBM Regional Office tofurnish the LGU copy of DA for the release of the excise tax in the prior years.

We recommended that management formulate a policy implementing guidelines for granting financial assistance and the basic requirements like letter request, barangay certification, and social case  study be attached to the disbursement voucher.
We also recommended the following information to be included in the Memorandum of Agreement:
Complete postal address of the grantee
Provision as to collectability/ liquidation of the grant
Monitoring of the success of the program
We recommended that the General Service Officer requires the submission of duly accomplished daily trip tickets before the use of government vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement and that for future similar transaction, the required supporting documents should accompany the Disbursement Voucher before payment to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office conduct a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.
We recommended that the concerned office submit the required documents as mandated by  COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts for post-audit.

We recommended that management expedite the implementation of the project so that beneficiaries would obtain maximum benefits from the government funds and to cope up with the timetable set by the donor agency.
We recommended that the management maximize the use of burley and tobacco excise tax so that the tobacco farmers would be fully benefitted from said fund.

We recommended that management require the persons liable to immediately settle the remaining suspensions and disallowances of P145,560.00. Settlement of the audit suspensions is through compliance with the requirements indicated for our evaluation. Items suspended in audit which are not settled within ninety (90) days from receipt shall become a disallowance pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts prescribed for use under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Audit Disallowances not appealed within 6 months becomes final and executory.
We recommended that management conduct physical count at the earliest possible time and to submit the required inventory report on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management create a policy implementing guidelines for granting financial assistance and the basic requirements like letter request, barangay certification, and social case  study be attached to the disbursement voucher.

We also recommended the following information to be included in the Memorandum of Agreement:

1.	Complete postal address of the grantee
2.	Provision as to collectability/ liquidation of the grant
3.	Monitoring of the success of the program
